# UC-18.3.21 · EVPN Ethernet Segment (ESI) DF Election and BUM Stability

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Fault, Availability &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch two paired switches that share one outward-facing cord so shouty Ethernet chatter travels predictably—when their microphone duty swaps wildly inside seconds we warn before broadcasts echo twice or vanish quietly.*

---

## Description

Ethernet Segment identifiers cement multi-homed CE posture across BGP-EVPN provider-edge routers per RFC 7432—every PE attaching to the same lag bundle shares one ES label so flood containment and aliasing-aware forwarding remain coherent. Designated Forwarder selection decides which PE injects broadcast, unknown unicast, and multicast floods per Ethernet Virtual Instance while siblings suppress duplicates via split-horizon tagging piggybacking Type-1 Ethernet Auto-Discovery routes and Type-4 Ethernet Segment Auto-Discovery advertisements declare DF eligibility among peers. When DF authority oscillates faster than convergence timers tolerate, CE-facing bundles hear contradictory flood paths—duplicate Ethernet frames circulate BUM domains or floods vanish altogether—and upstream aliasing shortcuts referencing Type-1 pointers stall so east-west hashing collapses toward DF-only egress despite All-Active intent. Operators therefore treat DF flap bursts as availability faults distinct from overlay tunnel resets because symptoms mimic silent duplication loss pairs rather than singular adjacency alarms.

## Value

Splunk aggregates normalized **`evpn:esi`** aggregates plus vendor **`show`** transcripts so NetOps ranks Ethernet Segments exhibiting rapid **`df_role`** churn versus steady **`esi`** partitions; dashboards expose VLAN-level **`vlans_count_as_df`** skew signaling Highest Random Weight imbalance versus modulo fairness targets; alerts correlate **`df_election_event_type`** bursts against BGP EVPN neighbor timelines isolating route-reflector churn from CE **`lacp`** suspend storms; drift monitors capture **`wait_seconds`** posture versus RFC 8584 defaults so shortened waits amplify flap sensitivity while elongated waits postpone legitimate failover; forensic joins stitch **`sonic:show:evpn`** snapshots with **`cisco:nxos:evpn`** counterparts proving mixed-vendor parity across **`esi_tag`** tuples—accelerating targeted rollback when DF instability threatens clustered workloads relying on symmetric hashing.

## Implementation

Deploy Splunkbase **2846**, 1918, **2847** parsers on Heavy Forwarders; schedule Ansible collectors stamping **`evpn:esi`** rows from **`show`** transcripts plus syslog-derived **`DF`** strings; normalize **`FIELDALIAS`** chains bridging **`esi`**, **`evi`**, **`local_pe_ip`**, **`df_role`** keys; publish **`esi_df_stability_core`** saved searches feeding dashboards routed via **`fabric_site`** ownership lookups.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- **Goal** — Maintain stable **DF** posture before **BUM** faults harm **CE** bundles.
- **Weekly cadence** — Compare **pressure_band** mixes week-over-week alongside **CHG** volume so creeping churn surfaces before CE bonds flap visibly.
- **Splunk TA footprint** — Deploy **Splunk Add-on for Cisco IOS/NX-OS** (**Splunkbase 2846**), **Splunk Add-on for Arista EOS** (**Splunkbase 1918**), and **Splunk Add-on for Juniper Networks** (**Splunkbase 2847**) on Heavy Forwarders plus parsing Indexers so **NX-OS**, **EOS**, and **Junos** syslog retains **PRI** fidelity before scripted collectors augment `show` transcripts landing `sonic:show:evpn` rows emitted via **vtysh** sessions targeting **FRRouting** **bgpd** containers on SONiC or Cumulus hosts.
- **Aggregator discipline** — Maintain `evpn:esi` sourcetype snapshots stamping `local_pe_ip`, `peer_pe_ip`, `esi`, `evi`, `df_role`, `df_election_event_type`, and `vlans_count_as_df` on **30–120s** cadences governed by Ansible inventories tying `leaf` identities to `fabric_site` ownership referenced downstream by dashboards and suppress lookups.
- **RBAC posture** — Grant `network_observer` roles `srchIndexesAllowed` spanning `dc_fabric`, `network_ops`, `cisco_dc`, `network`, and `os` indexes referenced inside `esi_df_stability_core` macros—avoid granting `admin` broadly to rotating responders unfamiliar with `Ethernet` `Segment` pager semantics.
- **TLS hygiene** — Prefer **REST**, **eAPI**, **NETCONF**, and **gNMI** collectors using **TLS** client certificates; rotate automation secrets quarterly and forbid personal **LDAP** passwords embedded inside modular inputs audited during **SOX**-style controls reviews when applicable.
- **Protocol anchors** — Document **RFC 7432** **ESI** rules (non-zero **ESI** implies multi-home) beside **RFC 8584** **DF** **wait** semantics so Splunk thresholds that read `wait_seconds` reference intentional knob movement rather than guessed defaults copied from forums.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) **Cisco Nexus 9000** — Poll **NX-OS** transcripts `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4`, `show evpn ethernet-segment`, and `show l2route evpn ethernet-segment all detail`, landing `cisco:nxos:evpn` rows with stable `Ethernet` `Segment` spellings needed for **FIELDALIAS** maps surviving minor **NX-OS** upgrades.
(2) **Arista EOS** — Fetch `show bgp evpn ethernet-segment` plus `show vxlan ethernet-segment` JSON through **eAPI** into `arista:eos:evpn`, keeping **CloudVision** hostnames aligned with scripted pulls to prevent duplicate `leaf_node` collisions during **stats** rollups.
(3) **Juniper Junos** — Combine `show evpn ethernet-segment` with `show route table bgp.evpn.0` extracts covering **Type 4** routes within `juniper:junos:evpn`, stamping `fabric_site` tags consistently with **Apstra** fabric names whenever **gNMI** streams also exist.
(4) **SONiC / FRRouting** — Serialize `show bgp l2vpn evpn ethernet-segment` JSON through **sonic-show** helpers into `sonic:show:evpn`, confirming **Docker** **bgpd** identities match **CMDB** **mgmt** names used across **inventory** lookups.
(5) **Aggregator merges** — Fuse vendor feeds into `evpn:esi` only after **QA** validates **FIELDALIAS** parity—especially `df_election_event_type` enumerations (**added**, **removed**, **changed**)—preserving modulo versus **HRW** comparability across platforms.

### Step 2 — Publish **esi_df_stability_core** saved search
Schedule `esi_df_stability_core` every **five minutes** with `earliest=-4h@m` embedded inside the saved object; throttle paging on `esi_tag`, `evi_tag`, and `leaf_node` tuples for **25 minutes** unless `churn_score` doubles—reducing duplicate tickets when `streamstats` windows overlap benign **show** bursts or **SC4S** syslog replays.

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=cisco_dc OR index=network OR index=os)
(sourcetype="evpn:esi" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:evpn" OR sourcetype="arista:eos:evpn" OR sourcetype="juniper:junos:evpn" OR sourcetype="sonic:show:evpn")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval leaf_node=trim(coalesce(leaf_hostname,hostname,switch_name,device_name,host,""))
| eval esi_tag=trim(coalesce(esi,ethernet_segment_id,esi_hex,esi_label,""))
| eval evi_tag=trim(coalesce(evi,evpn_instance,evpn_instance_id,evi_label,""))
| eval local_loopback=trim(coalesce(local_pe_ip,pe_local_ip,management_loopback,loopback0_ip,""))
| eval peer_loopback=trim(coalesce(peer_pe_ip,esi_peer_loopback,peer_loopback,""))
| eval df_label=lower(trim(coalesce(df_role,designated_forwarder_state,df_status,"")))
| eval df_transition=lower(trim(coalesce(df_election_event_type,df_change_reason,dfe_event,"")))
| eval vlan_df_share=tonumber(coalesce(vlans_count_as_df,vlan_df_total,vlan_df_hist,""))
| eval wait_seconds=tonumber(coalesce(df_wait_seconds,df_wait_timer_sec,rfc8584_wait,""))
| eval vendor_lane=case(match(sourcetype,"cisco:nxos:evpn"),"nxos_evpn_cli",match(sourcetype,"arista:eos:evpn"),"eos_evpn_cli",match(sourcetype,"juniper:junos:evpn"),"junos_evpn_cli",match(sourcetype,"sonic:show:evpn"),"sonic_vtysh_evpn",match(sourcetype,"evpn:esi"),"normalized_esi_agg","misc")
| eval split_hint=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(duplicate.*df|both.*forward|split.?brain|aliasing.*fail)"),"possible_peer_disagreement",null())
| eval rt4_echo=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(type.?\\s*4|esi.?route|segment.?auto)"),1,0)
| eval ead_echo=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(ead|type.?\\s*1)"),1,0)
| bin _time span=30s
| stats dc(df_label) AS df_state_entropy values(df_transition) AS transition_words values(split_hint) AS hint_words sum(rt4_echo) AS rt4_hits sum(ead_echo) AS ead_hits count AS evt_volume latest(local_loopback) AS local_pe latest(peer_loopback) AS peer_pe latest(vlan_df_share) AS vlan_df_latest latest(wait_seconds) AS wait_obs BY _time esi_tag evi_tag leaf_node vendor_lane
| eval vlan_bias_flag=if(isnotnull(vlan_df_latest) AND vlan_df_latest>85,"vlan_df_skew_high","vlan_df_nominal")
| eval churn_score=(evt_volume)+((df_state_entropy-1)*10)+(rt4_hits*2)+(ead_hits*2)
| eval peer_hint_hit=if(mvjoin(hint_words,"||") LIKE "%possible_peer_disagreement%",1,0)
| eval instability_family=case(churn_score>70 AND df_state_entropy>2,"df_role_flip_burst",vlan_bias_flag=="vlan_df_skew_high" AND evt_volume>8,"hash_skew_alarm",peer_hint_hit=1,"alias_split_hint",evt_volume>35,"election_noise_bucket",null())
| where isnotnull(instability_family)
| streamstats window=24 global=f sum(evt_volume) AS rolling_sum BY esi_tag evi_tag leaf_node
| eval pressure_band=case(rolling_sum>220,"high_pressure_tail",rolling_sum>110,"medium_pressure_tail","observed_tail")
| eval tenant_notes=mvjoin(transition_words,"|")
| sort - churn_score - rolling_sum
| head 420
| table _time leaf_node esi_tag evi_tag vendor_lane df_state_entropy evt_volume vlan_df_latest vlan_bias_flag churn_score instability_family rolling_sum pressure_band tenant_notes wait_obs local_pe peer_pe hint_words rt4_hits ead_hits
```

**SPL commentary** — `df_state_entropy` mirrors distinct `df_label` cardinality inside `stats` buckets—high entropy paired with `evt_volume` suggests oscillating DF posture risking duplicate **BUM** delivery.`vlan_bias_flag` highlights VLAN DF skew suspicion when `vlan_df_latest` crosses **85**, prompting **HRW** fairness investigations.`peer_hint_hit` aggregates syslog disagreement cues matched via `hint_words` — correlate with `wait_obs` to judge DF timer posture versus **RFC** **8584** expectations.`pressure_band` captures `rolling_sum` tails—helps prioritize `esi_tag` tuples with sustained churn versus sporadic spikes.`tenant_notes` retains `values(df_transition)` snippets valuable when aligning `MaintenanceWindow` overlays with `knownFalsePositives` tokens documented in JSON.

### Step 3 — Validate against vendor parity drills
SSH into `leaf_node` exemplars surfaced by `Splunk` panels; compare `esi_tag`/`evi_tag` pairs against `show` transcripts enumerated in Step 1 ensuring `evt_volume` aligns with CLI-visible DF transitions within polling drift.**Junos** installs reconcile `bgp.evpn.0` Type-4 winners against `peer_pe` loopbacks surfaced by `Splunk`.**EOS** peers validate `show vxlan ethernet-segment` DF columns versus `vendor_lane` tagging.**SONiC** compares **Docker** timestamps against **_time** deltas—large skew implies indexer lag rather than fabric churn.Cross-check `expected_esi.csv` design lookups whenever parsers emit suspicious zero **ESI** readings despite visually multi-homed cabling.

### Step 4 — Operationalize dashboards and escalation ladders
Dashboard Studio workspace `Ethernet Segment DF Stability` stacks `heat-map` `esi_tag` × `leaf_node` facets colored by `churn_score` alongside `timechart` `evt_volume` lanes segmented via `instability_family` overlays annotated `pressure_band` thresholds.Alert routing pushes `hash_skew_alarm` rows toward capacity councils weighing **HRW** fairness—routes `alias_split_hint` toward **BGP** SMEs correlating `RR` maintenance tickets—routes `df_role_flip_burst` toward Ethernet fabric owners keyed off `esi_tag` CMDB stewardship. Embed `MaintenanceWindow` overlays tie `CHG` identifiers to suppress macros honoring `knownFalsePositives` narratives—prevent noisy repeats whenever `df_algorithm_change` tickets remain open legitimately.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting modes

- **Silent panels despite CLI DF drama** — widen `coalesce` inputs after `fieldsummary` exposes renamed telemetry keys—confirm `earliest` ranges cover incidents—and verify Splunk roles retain `srchIndexesAllowed` parity across `dc_fabric` unions referenced inside macros.

- **Persistent `election_noise_bucket` positives** — revisit suppress lookups referencing `df_wait_timer_change`, `esi_participant_addremove`, `df_algorithm_change`, and `evpn_rr_cluster_maintenance` contexts documented beside ticketing bridges—pager fatigue drops once `CHG` closures align with `Splunk` timelines.

- **`hash_skew_alarm` without VLAN imbalance on CLI** — inspect raw `vlans_count_as_df` counts versus VLAN totals exported nightly—repair **FIELDALIAS** mappings rather than loosening SPL thresholds prematurely—often parser drift not HRW imbalance.

- **`peer_hint_hit` stuck at zero** — broaden `split_hint` regex cautiously using archived `_raw` samples reviewed quarterly—avoid unconstrained regex doubling EPS costs during BGP storms coinciding unrelated `withdraw` bursts monitored elsewhere.

- **`rolling_sum` explosions absent CE symptoms** — duplicate syslog ingestion doubles `evt_volume`—audit **SC4S** relays for cloned TCP taps—dedupe `host` keys prior to raising thresholds blindly upstream.

- **`vendor_lane` imbalance skews aggregates** — clone searches filtering `nxos_evpn_cli`, `eos_evpn_cli`, `junos_evpn_cli`, `sonic_vtysh_evpn`, or `normalized_esi_agg` individually when heterogeneous automation maturity renders blended `churn_score` comparisons misleading across sibling pods.

## SPL

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=cisco_dc OR index=network OR index=os)
(sourcetype="evpn:esi" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:evpn" OR sourcetype="arista:eos:evpn" OR sourcetype="juniper:junos:evpn" OR sourcetype="sonic:show:evpn")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval leaf_node=trim(coalesce(leaf_hostname,hostname,switch_name,device_name,host,""))
| eval esi_tag=trim(coalesce(esi,ethernet_segment_id,esi_hex,esi_label,""))
| eval evi_tag=trim(coalesce(evi,evpn_instance,evpn_instance_id,evi_label,""))
| eval local_loopback=trim(coalesce(local_pe_ip,pe_local_ip,management_loopback,loopback0_ip,""))
| eval peer_loopback=trim(coalesce(peer_pe_ip,esi_peer_loopback,peer_loopback,""))
| eval df_label=lower(trim(coalesce(df_role,designated_forwarder_state,df_status,"")))
| eval df_transition=lower(trim(coalesce(df_election_event_type,df_change_reason,dfe_event,"")))
| eval vlan_df_share=tonumber(coalesce(vlans_count_as_df,vlan_df_total,vlan_df_hist,""))
| eval wait_seconds=tonumber(coalesce(df_wait_seconds,df_wait_timer_sec,rfc8584_wait,""))
| eval vendor_lane=case(match(sourcetype,"cisco:nxos:evpn"),"nxos_evpn_cli",match(sourcetype,"arista:eos:evpn"),"eos_evpn_cli",match(sourcetype,"juniper:junos:evpn"),"junos_evpn_cli",match(sourcetype,"sonic:show:evpn"),"sonic_vtysh_evpn",match(sourcetype,"evpn:esi"),"normalized_esi_agg","misc")
| eval split_hint=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(duplicate.*df|both.*forward|split.?brain|aliasing.*fail)"),"possible_peer_disagreement",null())
| eval rt4_echo=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(type.?\s*4|esi.?route|segment.?auto)"),1,0)
| eval ead_echo=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(ead|type.?\s*1)"),1,0)
| bin _time span=30s
| stats dc(df_label) AS df_state_entropy values(df_transition) AS transition_words values(split_hint) AS hint_words sum(rt4_echo) AS rt4_hits sum(ead_echo) AS ead_hits count AS evt_volume latest(local_loopback) AS local_pe latest(peer_loopback) AS peer_pe latest(vlan_df_share) AS vlan_df_latest latest(wait_seconds) AS wait_obs BY _time esi_tag evi_tag leaf_node vendor_lane
| eval vlan_bias_flag=if(isnotnull(vlan_df_latest) AND vlan_df_latest>85,"vlan_df_skew_high","vlan_df_nominal")
| eval churn_score=(evt_volume)+((df_state_entropy-1)*10)+(rt4_hits*2)+(ead_hits*2)
| eval peer_hint_hit=if(mvjoin(hint_words,"||") LIKE "%possible_peer_disagreement%",1,0)
| eval instability_family=case(churn_score>70 AND df_state_entropy>2,"df_role_flip_burst",vlan_bias_flag=="vlan_df_skew_high" AND evt_volume>8,"hash_skew_alarm",peer_hint_hit=1,"alias_split_hint",evt_volume>35,"election_noise_bucket",null())
| where isnotnull(instability_family)
| streamstats window=24 global=f sum(evt_volume) AS rolling_sum BY esi_tag evi_tag leaf_node
| eval pressure_band=case(rolling_sum>220,"high_pressure_tail",rolling_sum>110,"medium_pressure_tail","observed_tail")
| eval tenant_notes=mvjoin(transition_words,"|")
| sort - churn_score - rolling_sum
| head 420
| table _time leaf_node esi_tag evi_tag vendor_lane df_state_entropy evt_volume vlan_df_latest vlan_bias_flag churn_score instability_family rolling_sum pressure_band tenant_notes wait_obs local_pe peer_pe hint_words rt4_hits ead_hits
```

## Visualization

(1) **Heat-map** — **`leaf_node`** × **`esi_tag`** colored by **`churn_score`**. (2) **Timechart** — **`evt_volume`** stacked by **`instability_family`**. (3) **Scatter** — **`vlan_df_latest`** versus **`df_state_entropy`**. (4) **Table** — worst **`esi_tag`** rows listing **`pressure_band`**, **`tenant_notes`**, **`peer_pe`**. (5) **Single-value** tiles summarizing **`vlan_bias_flag`** skew counts across **`fabric_site`**.

## Known False Positives

**lacp_system_id_rotation** — CE operators reshuffle **`lacp`** bundle **`system-id`** values during cabling tidy-ups—brief Type-1 Ethernet Auto-Discovery churn resembles **`esi`** flap though BGP stays **`Established`**—suppress positives referencing **`lacp_system_id_rotation`** change tickets paired with **`MaintenanceWindow`** overlays until **`lag`** settles.

**esi_participant_addremove** — Engineering inserts another PE serving the same **`Ethernet`** **`Segment`**—DF winners reshuffle deliberately—tie **`esi_participant_addremove`** CAB receipts to Splunk timelines before paging **`instability_family`** bursts lacking outage symptoms.

**evpn_rr_cluster_maintenance** — Spinning **`BGP`** **`route-reflector`** clusters forces Type-4 Ethernet Segment Auto-Discovery readvertisement storms unrelated to CE faults—honor **`evpn_rr_cluster_maintenance`** **`CHG`** identifiers describing **`RR`** rotations alongside **`BGP`** **`Established`** uptime parity checks.

**df_wait_timer_change** — Operators lengthen **`RFC`** **`8584`** **`DF`** **`wait`** timers (**seconds**) calming hypersensitive elections—Splunk captures elevated **`wait_obs`** counters—suppress **`election_noise_bucket`** positives labeled **`df_wait_timer_change`** inside **`runbook`** annotations referencing timer deltas explicitly.

**lacp_system_priority_change** — CE-side **`lacp`** **`system-priority`** edits reorder bundle **`actor`** selection causing transient **`EAD`** withdrawals resembling PE instability—pair syslog **`lacp_system_priority_change`** narratives with **`evt_volume`** dips before escalating **`df_role_flip_burst`** alarms alone.

**ce_bond_firmware_upgrade** — Hypervisor **`NIC`** firmware pushes **`suspend`** **`lag`** members momentarily—all PE routers observe withdrawn Type-1 routes concurrently—suppress **`alias_split_hint`** markers referencing **`ce_bond_firmware_upgrade`** **`CHG`** bundles tied to clustered **`NIC`** inventories.

**df_algorithm_change** — Migrating modulo **`DF`** hashing toward Highest Random Weight reshuffles VLAN **`DF`** winners overnight—expect benign spikes until **`vlan_df_latest`** histograms stabilize—annotate dashboards with **`df_algorithm_change`** **`CHG`** metadata.

**route_target_adjustment_propagation** — **`VRF`** **`route-target`** edits ripple EVPN **`RT`** imports briefly shaking **`EAD`** **`ES`** **`routes`**—differentiate via ticketing tokens **`route_target_adjustment_propagation`** referencing **`policy`** edits lacking **`esi_tag`** persistence faults beyond minutes.

**vpc_peer_link_reload_isolation** — **`vPC`** **`peer-link`** reloads asymmetrically starve one **`Nexus`** PE while optics elsewhere remain **`up`**—Splunk logs **`esi`** chatter resembling splits—suppress **`possible_peer_disagreement`** hints mapped to **`vpc_peer_link_reload_isolation`** maintenance bridges documented alongside **`ISSU`** choreography.

## References

- [IETF RFC 7432 — BGP MPLS-Based Ethernet VPN](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7432)
- [IETF RFC 8584 — BGP Ethernet VPN — Efficient Handling of Broadcast, Unknown Unicast and Multicast (BUM) Traffic](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8584)
- [Cisco Nexus 9000 Series NX-OS BGP EVPN Configuration Guide](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/dcn/nx-os/nexus9000/103x/configuration/evpn/)
- [Splunk Lantern — practice guidance library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)
- [Splunk Add-on for Cisco IOS and NX-OS (Splunkbase 2846)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2846)
